commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Telegram bot-to-bot admission policy/config object, applies it before inbound Telegram authorization, updates channel_post fixtures, and adjusts one polling-session test.

PR surface: Source +223, Tests +230. Total +453 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the patch blockers: source inspection of the PR head shows the default-disabled gate before existing inbound authorization, username-only admission, and missing docs/metadata. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the remaining blockers combine Telegram product/security config direction, channel_post upgrade behavior, and contributor real-behavior proof, not a safe automated repair.

target.title: fix(memory): respect QMD timeout for memory_search
target.body:
## Summary
- apply `memory.qmd.limits.timeoutMs` only to actual QMD-backed `memory_search` work
- keep the existing 15s guard for manager setup, wiki/supplement-only searches, builtin searches, and QMD-to-builtin fallback paths
- strengthen regression coverage for raised-QMD-timeout behavior across QMD search, `corpus="wiki"`, and fallback searches
- document that QMD `limits.timeoutMs` controls the QMD memory-search budget and add a manual smoke test for checking gateway/backend logs after raising the timeout

## What Problem This Solves
QMD-backed memory searches can legitimately take longer than the historical fixed 15s `memory_search` wrapper, even though the QMD config already exposes `memory.qmd.limits.timeoutMs`. The original fix made that configured timeout the whole-tool deadline too early, which also lengthened non-QMD work such as wiki-only supplement searches and builtin/fallback searches. This revision keeps the longer QMD budget scoped to live QMD-backed memory-search work while preserving the old 15s guard everywhere else.

## Evidence
- Local QMD-backed runtime evidence from the PR thread shows the pre-patch fixed 15s timeout and a post-patch QMD result completing with `searchMs: 23922` under `memory.qmd.limits.timeoutMs = 45000`.
- Added focused fake-timer coverage proving a configured 45s QMD search is still pending and un-aborted at 15s, then aborts with `memory_search timed out after 45s`.
- Added focused fake-timer coverage proving a QMD-configured `corpus="wiki"` supplement-only call still returns `memory_search timed out after 15s` and never opens the memory manager.
- Added focused fake-timer coverage proving a QMD-configured live builtin fallback search still returns `memory_search timed out after 15s` and aborts the search signal.
- Local validation on this branch passed: `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory-tool-manager.test-mocks.ts`.
- Local validation on this branch passed: `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/concepts/memory-qmd.md docs/reference/memory-config.md`.
- Local validation on this branch passed: `node scripts/test-extension.mjs memory-core extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts` with 2 files and 40 tests passing.
- Local validation on this branch passed: `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`.
- Local validation on this branch passed: `node scripts/test-extension.mjs memory-core` with 63 files and 915 tests passing.

## Manual smoke test
After applying this branch to an installation with QMD memory enabled:
1. Set `memory.qmd.limits.timeoutMs` above 15000, e.g. `45000`, and restart the gateway.
2. Run `memory_search` from a tool-enabled agent session with a query known to take longer than 15s on that machine.
3. Inspect gateway/backend logs for that search window. The old `memory_search timed out after 15s` failure should not appear for the QMD-backed search. If the search still exceeds the configured QMD budget, the unavailable payload should report the configured timeout, e.g. `memory_search timed out after 45s`.
4. Run a wiki-only or fallback-path search under the same raised QMD timeout. Those non-QMD paths should still hit the existing 15s guard if they stall.

## Tests
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory-tool-manager.test-mocks.ts`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/concepts/memory-qmd.md docs/reference/memory-config.md`
- `node scripts/test-extension.mjs memory-core extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `node scripts/test-extension.mjs memory-core`
target.author: bdjben
